item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for taking the time to comment, DrUnoriginal.

The either was left over from a last minute edit, I'll go change that in a sec. The third point is an attempt to capture a dialect rather than a mistake. I'll have a think about the first - I thought it said what I wanted it to, but know I'm doubting myself!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm. This is quite in a weird place for me. The dialogue's quite natural, and I think that's been a big plus for every article you've done, so that's very good, to start off. The ending felt… a little sweet, but, hm, like, I wasn't completely sure on what else to feel and get from it? The "twist" about the grass being anomalous and that it conveys the emotions of the person giving to another sounds interesting, though it didn't really build up much and just… ended? So the ending felt a little iffy, imo. Novote.

+ very nitpicky stuff
- hide

monitored for any anomalous activity unassociated with known SCP-5530 instances

Is it meant to be "unassociated"? Or is this trying to say like, watch out for some strange events that happen; like the person was either struck by good/bad luck because of the charm? Cuz if it is this, then I'm not very sure if these events are necessarily considered "unassociated" because some of these are perhaps caused by the charm?

until either that instance is destroyed.

Hm. That "either" reads a little weird to me? Because there's no like, "choice"? Like, "either this or that"?

He gave it me and off he marched

I think it's missing a "to" after "it"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the feedback, Rounderhouse. I'll bear all of that in mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't really have an emotional core, though it tries its damnedest. The interviews are functionally just exposition dumps, and the effect of the anomaly is pretty apparent by the time you get to the lady with 16 kids. The whole throughline of this piece is emotional attachment… except the reader is never really given a reason to care. There's no attempt made to engage them; the person I'm supposed to feel bad for has all of 5 lines and no other appearance in the article. It's just longer than it needs to be, and culminates in trying to elicit an emotional response without putting in the characterization or atmosphere needed for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huge thanks to Sonderance, Popsioak, and everyone in IRC I've pestered to take a look.
